transcript:
I goes to the store yesterday. She don't like apples. 
We has a meeting tomorrow. He have a nice car. 
I goes shopping every week.
full_transcript:
I goes to the store yesterday. She don't like apples. 
We has a meeting tomorrow. He have a nice car. 
I goes shopping every week.

errors.message: The pronoun ‘I’ must be used with a non-third-person form of a verb.
errors.context: I goes to the store yesterday. She don't like ...

cambridge.title: Subject-Verb Agreement: I/You/We/They + base form
cambridge.explanation: Use the base form of the verb (without -s) with I, you, we, and they.

errors.message: The pronoun ‘She’ is usually used with a third-person or a past tense verb.
errors.context: I goes to the store yesterday. She don't like apples.  We has a meeting tomor...

cambridge.title: Subject-Verb Agreement: He/She/It + verb-s
cambridge.explanation: Add -s/-es to verbs in Present Simple with he, she, it.
examples: ❌ She don't like apples → She doesn't like apples

errors.message: The pronoun ‘We’ must be used with a non-third-person form of a verb.
errors.context: ...e yesterday. She don't like apples.  We has a meeting tomorrow. He have a nice car....

cambridge.title: Subject-Verb Agreement: I/You/We/They + base form
cambridge.explanation: Use the base form of the verb (without -s) with I, you, we, and they.

errors.message: The pronoun ‘He’ is usually used with a third-person or a past tense verb.
errors.context: ... apples.  We has a meeting tomorrow. He have a nice car.  I goes shopping every week...

cambridge.title: Subject-Verb Agreement: He/She/It + verb-s
cambridge.explanation: Add -s/-es to verbs in Present Simple with he, she, it.
examples: ❌ She don't like apples → She doesn't like apples
